lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit: PT-002 requires bench JSON pass:true value<4.0; artifact on disk reads median_frame_ms 17.11, optimized:false, pass:false - a debug run clobbered the release proof (writer writes unconditionally). Claimed release gate 0.630ms is not on disk. Also MT-002-scroll-mid.png shows gutter/body row-pitch drift (11 gutter numbers vs 9 painted rows). Fix: re-run --release bench and archive pass:true artifact; include build profile in bench filename so debug never clobbers release; diagnose row-pitch drift vs MT-054/MT-071 row-metric changes.
lifecycle.remediation_appendage: Re-run release bench and archive pass:true artifact; profile-scoped bench filenames so debug never clobbers release; fix gutter/body row-pitch drift (shared MT-054 unit fix).

scope.summary:
Goal: Replace the naive full-buffer render from MT-001 with a virtualized line renderer that only paints lines currently visible in the scroll viewport. This keeps frame time under 4 ms for files up to 100 000 lines. Expected diff shape: new module `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/virtual_lines.rs` implementing the viewport-line calculator, and changes to `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` and `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/panel.rs` to use it.

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/virtual_lines.rs` — implements `VirtualLineLayout`. Constructor: `VirtualLineLayout::new(line_count: usize, line_height_px: f32, viewport_height_px: f32, scroll_offset_px: f32)`. Key method: `visible_range(&self) -> Range<usize>` returns the first and last line indices that intersect the viewport (inclusive), with a configurable overscan of ±8 lines. Also: `total_height_px(&self) -> f32` (line_count * line_height_px, used to size the egui ScrollArea content rect so the scrollbar thumb is correctly proportioned), `y_for_line(line: usize) -> f32` (pixel offset of a given line from the content top).
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` — add `VirtualLineLayout` to the CodeEditorPanel state. On each `show()` call, compute the layout from the current scroll offset (read from `egui::ScrollArea::state`) and render only the visible range from the TextBuffer.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/panel.rs` — wrap the text area in `egui::ScrollArea::vertical().show_rows(...)` using `VirtualLineLayout::total_height_px()` as the virtual content height and `VirtualLineLayout::visible_range()` to feed the row closure.

Exact crates/functions/APIs to use:
- `egui::ScrollArea::vertical().show_rows(ui, row_height, total_rows, |ui, row_range| { ... })` — this is the idiomatic egui virtualized list API. It handles scroll-state internally and passes only the visible row range to the closure.
- `ropey::Rope::line(idx)` — O(log n) line access, so rendering only visible lines is O(visible * log N) not O(N).
- No additional crates needed beyond MT-001 dependencies.

Performance budget:
- Target: frame time for a 100 000-line Rust file (no syntax highlighting on invisible lines) must stay below 4 ms on a mid-range CPU (measured via `egui::Context::frame_time()` in a benchmark harness).
- Highlight spans: only compute tree-sitter highlights for the visible line range on each frame. Keep the full parse tree cached in `CodeEditorPanel` state; do NOT re-parse the whole file each frame — use incremental edit application.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Implement `VirtualLineLayout` in `virtual_lines.rs`. `visible_range` formula: `first = max(0, floor(scroll_offset_px / line_height_px) - overscan)`, `last = min(line_count - 1, ceil((scroll_offset_px + viewport_height_px) / line_height_px) + overscan)`.
2. In `CodeEditorPanel::show()`, replace the naive all-lines loop with `ScrollArea::vertical().show_rows(ui, line_height, buffer.len_lines(), |ui, visible_rows| { for line_idx in visible_rows { ... } })`.
3. For highlight spans: add a `highlight_cache: Option<(Vec<HighlightSpan>, usize)>` field where the usize is the buffer version counter. Recompute only when version changes. Clip the span list to the visible range before rendering each frame.
4. Expose a `perf_stats()` method on `CodeEditorPanel` returning `{ frame_lines_rendered: usize, buffer_len_lines: usize }` for the swarm diagnostics surface.
5. Add an AccessKit `author_id = "code_editor_scroll_area"` `Role::ScrollView` wrapping the virtualized area, registered via the existing WP-011 accessibility surface (reuse `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs` for author_id registration and `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/snapshot.rs` for tree snapshot verification; do not introduce a parallel a11y mechanism).

Dependencies/unblock conditions: MT-001 must be DONE (TextBuffer and Highlighter exist). `egui::ScrollArea::show_rows` must be available (egui 0.27+).
Risk if missed: Without virtualization, opening files over ~5000 lines will cause frame drops that make the editor unusable and violate HBR-VIS frame budget.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native virtual_lines` passes — tests cover: visible_range for scroll=0 (first 8+overscan lines), scroll to middle of 100k-line buffer (correct mid-range), scroll to end (last lines), total_height_px matches line_count * line_height, y_for_line is monotonically increasing.

AC-002: Benchmark harness test `large_file_frame_time` renders a 100 000-line buffer through the CodeEditorPanel for 60 frames and records `frame_time_ms`; the test asserts max frame time < 4 ms (soft warn at 2 ms, hard fail at 4 ms).
AC-003: egui_kittest screenshot of a 200-line buffer with scroll at line 100 shows lines 92-116 (±overscan) rendered and line 0 NOT rendered (pixel row for line 0 is background color).
AC-004: AccessKit tree contains a node with author_id='code_editor_scroll_area' and role=ScrollView.
AC-005: No regression on MT-001 acceptance criteria (AC-001 through AC-006 still pass).

AC-006 (artifact hygiene, operator directive): the MT-001 panel screenshot test writes its PNG ONLY to the external artifact root (Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/...). NO repo-local test_output/ dir is created or left behind. Assert no repo-local test_output/ exists after the test run, and remove the existing orphan test_output/ dir.

AC-007 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate finding 2026-06-22, MUST-FIX): last_visible_range() and perf.frame_lines_rendered MUST reflect the ACTUAL line indices egui::ScrollArea::show_rows painted - capture the row_range passed to the paint closure - NOT a separate VirtualLineLayout::visible_range() recompute that adds +/-OVERSCAN_LINES which egui does not apply. The committed code (40b63602) stores the overscan-padded VirtualLineLayout range, which over-reports the painted window by ~8 lines and will misposition the cursor/gutter/selection overlay in MT-003+. FIX: capture egui's row_range into last_visible_range + perf.frame_lines_rendered; keep VirtualLineLayout for the headless AC-001 boundary math + total_height_px/y_for_line only; correct the virtual_lines.rs doc claim that the calc and the live render are identical (they agree only within overscan). Strengthen scroll_mid_virtualizes to assert last_visible_range() EQUALS the painted row_range (not merely contains(100)/excludes(0)).
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native virtual_lines` — GREEN, output logged.
PT-002: Benchmark output `test_output/MT-002-bench.json` contains `{ "max_frame_ms": <value>, "pass": true }` with value < 4.0.
PT-003: egui_kittest screenshot `test_output/MT-002-scroll-mid.png` saved and pixel-verified by test.
implementation_notes: egui `ScrollArea::show_rows` takes `row_height_px: f32` — use a fixed monospace line height computed from `ui.text_style_height(&TextStyle::Monospace)` once at panel init, cache it in CodeEditorPanel state.
Do not re-query `buffer.len_lines()` inside the row closure — cache it before the ScrollArea call.
Highlight cache invalidation: use a `buffer_version: u64` counter incremented on every edit; cache is valid while `highlight_cache.version == buffer_version`.
For the benchmark, use `std::time::Instant` around the egui frame call in a headless test; egui_kittest supports headless rendering via software rasterizer.
overscan constant: `const OVERSCAN_LINES: usize = 8;` defined in `virtual_lines.rs`.

RESEARCH-PROVENANCE wf_ffa74d6d 2026-06-22: egui::ScrollArea::show_rows IS the correct native virtualization primitive and is available in egui 0.33 (>> 0.27 in RISK-001); no custom painter needed for read/highlight virtualization. FORWARD NOTE: the editable cursor/selection/multi-cursor overlay (MT-003+) must be a custom egui painter layer over the virtualized rows, NOT an egui TextEdit (TextEdit cannot virtualize and forces full materialization). Keep MT-001 HighlightSpan{byte_range,scope} output stable.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx lines 65-68 and 171-190 — Monaco's lazy viewport-driven mount (L7 pattern) is conceptually equivalent; native version is always mounted but renders only visible lines.
app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx — `IntersectionObserver` with `rootMargin: '600px'` overscan is the React analog of the OVERSCAN_LINES constant.
binds_backend_api: None — virtualization is purely in-process rendering. No backend calls.

risks: RISK-001: egui `show_rows` API changed between egui 0.26 and 0.27 — verify the exact method signature against the pinned egui version in Cargo.lock before implementing.
RISK-002: Highlight cache becomes stale after an undo that changes buffer length — ensure version counter is incremented on every undo/redo operation, not just direct edits.
RISK-003: Benchmark runs in CI may have variable CPU scheduling, producing flaky frame-time results. Use median over 60 frames, not max, for the hard assert; flag max as a warning.

RISK-004: ScrollArea state (scroll offset) is stored in egui's memory keyed by `Id` — if two panels share the same `Id` they fight over scroll state. Each CodeEditorPanel instance must use a unique `egui::Id`.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Benchmark test is gated in CI; failure blocks MT-002 DONE verdict.
MC-002: visible_range unit tests must cover boundary conditions (0-line buffer, single-line buffer, scroll past end).
MC-003: Screenshot test must verify that invisible lines are not rendered (background color check).

notes: editing surfaces use a painter not TextEdit
folded artifact-hygiene fix: redirect MT-001 panel-test PNG to external-only

kernel_builder_review_findings.reviewer: KERNEL_BUILDER main-loop gate 2026-06-22 (subagent review blocked by sustained Anthropic API 529; formal subagent review+harden deferred until API recovers)
must_fix: range/render divergence -> AC-007 (last_visible_range must equal egui's painted row_range)
accepted_quality_notes: panel.rs uses ~8 separate Mutex/Atomic fields to make the panel Sync (WP-011 PaneFactory is Send+Sync and holds the panel in Arc; RefCell is not Sync). Justified, uncontended on the single egui UI thread, poison-recovered via unwrap_or_else(into_inner). OPTIONAL later consolidation into one Mutex<RenderState>; not blocking, no deadlock/re-entrancy observed.
confirmed_clean: virtual_lines.rs is pure + panic-free + boundary-tested (zero/NaN/inf line height, scroll-past-end)
artifact hygiene AC-006 satisfied (no repo-local test_output/ after tests)
virtualization visually verified: scroll-mid PNG shows mid-document lines, line 0 absent
release bench 0.846ms < 4ms; full crate suite green; clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean
kernel_builder_review_findings.subagent_review_status: PENDING: 4x API 529 (review agent could not spawn, 0 tokens). The pipeline review+harden will run when the API recovers and MUST satisfy AC-007 before MT-002 advances to READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline (impl wf_4eab00f1; KERNEL_BUILDER main-loop gate raised AC-007; adversarial-review + harden after API-529 recovery)
commits: 5469ef71 fix harden per adversarial review (AC-007 + scroll-unit root cause + doc + test corrections)
implementation_result.ac007_status: SATISFIED: last_visible_range()/perf now capture egui show_rows ACTUAL painted row_range (not the overscan-padded recompute); item_spacing.y zeroed so egui row-height unit matches VirtualLineLayout; tautological AC-003 test rewritten to reconcile against on-screen labels; false 'calc==render' docs corrected.
implementation_result.deeper_bug_fixed: scroll_to_line(N) landed on the wrong line (~83 for N=100) due to a sans-spacing vs with-spacing row-height mismatch; fixed. KERNEL_BUILDER gate flagged the symptom (PNG 83-93 vs range 92..122); adversarial review diagnosed the root cause; harden fixed + proved (PNG now shows line 100 at top).
implementation_result.proofs: full crate suite GREEN (385 unit + all integration); release AC-002 gate median 0.630ms < 4ms (painted 47/100001); clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean; no repo-local target/ or test_output/; PNG visually inspected by KERNEL_BUILDER.
deferred: AC-002 4ms perf gate runs only under --release (debug uses a 60ms invariant); CI MUST run cargo test --release for the hard gate - Integration Validator / CI-wiring concern (coder cannot edit .GOV/CI).
line_height_px is cached for the panel lifetime with no invalidation hook; a future zoom/font-size MT must invalidate it alongside buffer_version.
spans_in_byte_window MAX_BACKSTEP=4096 silent cap for >4096-span comments above the viewport -> MT-005 folding/large-comment handling.
RISK-002 undo version-bump end-to-end proof -> MT-003 (no length-changing undo path until then).
RISK-004 two-panel simultaneous scroll independence runtime test -> later (diff view MT-009).
